rss.title: Iranian missiles injure 160 in towns near Israeli nuclear site
rss.snippet: Israel is investigating how ballistic missiles got through the country's sophisticated air defences.

rss.title: Sudan drone attack on key hospital killed 64 people during Eid, WHO says
rss.snippet: The strike killed 64 people, including 13 children, two nurses and a doctor, according to the WHO head.

rss.title: National blackout hits Cuba for second time in a week
rss.snippet: Another total failure of its power grid underlines Cuba's hardship under a US-imposed fuel blockade.

arxiv_papers.title: Grievance Politics vs. Policy Debates: A Cross-Platform Analysis of Conservative Discourse on Truth Social and Reddit
arxiv_papers.snippet: We present the first large-scale comparative analysis of Truth Social and the most popular conservative Reddit communities, r/Conservative, r/conservatives, and r/Republican. Using topic modeling with FASTopic and LLM-assisted refinement, we analyze topic prevalence, toxicity, and temporal dynamics

arxiv_papers.title: A Multi-Level Data-driven Framework for Understanding Perceptions Towards Cycling Infrastructure Across Regions Leveraging Social Media Discourse
arxiv_papers.snippet: Cycling plays an important role in sustainable urban mobility, yet how people perceive cycling infrastructure varies widely and remains challenging to assess at large spatial scales. Existing research has mainly relied on surveys or short-form social media data and has often focused on individual ci

podcast: [Don't hate the replicator, hate the game] The world of science has been stuck in an existential crisis over whether we actually know the things we thought we knew. Re-running an old study today doesn't always yield the same result. Same with re-enacting old experiments.
